Title: Elucidation of the biosynthetic pathway of hydroxysafflor yellow A

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Carthamus tinctorius; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Hydroxysafflor yellow A (HSYA) biosynthesis; flavonoid specialized metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Upstream hydroxylation branch leading from naringenin to carthamidin
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes 6-hydroxylation of flavanones/flavones, converting naringenin to 6-hydroxynaringenin (carthamidin) and apigenin to scutellarein.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: flowers, especially budding flowers of safflower
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: clinical investigational new drug lead for acute ischemic stroke; alleviates atherosclerosis, cerebral ischemia/reperfusion injury, myocardial ischemia, vascular injury, alcohol-induced liver injury, and diabetes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes HSYA accumulation by supplying carthamidin, an essential precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtF6H was identified as a flavanone 6-hydroxylase from safflower. 2. It catalyzed 6-hydroxylation in vitro, including conversion of naringenin to carthamidin and apigenin to scutellarein. 3. Silencing CtF6H in safflower reduced HSYA content by 30.8%. 4. Together with CtCGT and Ct2OGD1, it enabled de novo HSYA biosynthesis in Nicotiana benthamiana.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in budding flowers; very low in white-flower safflower variant
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Budding flowers in safflower; heterologously expressed in N. benthamiana leaves and yeast microsomes
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro microsomal enzyme assay; VIGS in Carthamus tinctorius; transient expression/de novo pathway reconstruction in Nicotiana benthamiana; protein interaction assays (SFLC, BiFC)
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Budding flower developmental stage; VIGS treatment; transient Agrobacterium infiltration; substrate feeding
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key pathway enzyme, not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential gene for reconstructing HSYA biosynthesis; should be co-expressed with CtCGT, Ct2OGD1, and CtCHI1, and high expression is required for production.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CtF6H homolog replacement by SbF6H could support HSYA formation from carthamidin in N. benthamiana. Lack of high CtF6H expression explains failure of a white-flower safflower variant to accumulate HSYA.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Carthamus tinctorius; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: UDP-dependent glycosyltransferase, UGT708 family, type I CGT with DPF motif
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: phloretin; 2-hydroxynaringenin; carthamidin or isocarthamidin with Ct2OGD1 in coupled reaction
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: nothofagin; 3′,5′-di-C-glycosylphloretin; 2-hydroxynaringenin mono-C-glycoside; 6,8-di-C-glycosylated 2-hydroxynaringenin; HSYA in coordination with Ct2OGD1
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes flavonoid C-glycosylation, including mono- and di-C-glycosylation of phloretin and 2-hydroxynaringenin using UDP-Glc; coordinates with Ct2OGD1 to accomplish the final HSYA-forming reaction from carthamidin/isocarthamidin-derived intermediate(s).
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product in coordination with Ct2OGD1
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: flowers, especially budding flowers of safflower
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: clinical investigational new drug lead for acute ischemic stroke; multiple protective bioactivities
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for HSYA biosynthesis; reduction lowers HSYA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtCGT is a safflower UGT708 family flavonoid di-C-glycosyltransferase. 2. It glycosylated phloretin and 2-hydroxynaringenin in vitro but not naringenin or naringenin chalcone. 3. VIGS of CtCGT reduced HSYA content by 29.6% in safflower. 4. CtCGT physically interacted with Ct2OGD1 and, together with Ct2OGD1, catalyzed the last step of HSYA biosynthesis. 5. Inclusion of CtCHI1 improved HSYA yield in coupled systems.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Budding flowers in safflower; heterologously expressed in N. benthamiana leaves and yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purified enzyme assays; VIGS in Carthamus tinctorius; transient expression and de novo biosynthesis in Nicotiana benthamiana; yeast semi-synthesis; SFLC; BiFC; MST
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Budding flower developmental stage; VIGS; transient Agrobacterium infiltration; substrate feeding
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key terminal module enzyme, not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Competing F2H pathway can divert precursor flux to common flavone C-glycosides instead of HSYA
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Core engineering target for HSYA production; should be combined with CtF6H, Ct2OGD1, and preferably CtCHI1; avoid introducing F2H activity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CtCGT was named UGT708U8 by the UGT Nomenclature Committee. It showed optimum activity at pH 9.0 and 45 °C, metal-ion independent for standalone glycosylation assays.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Carthamus tinctorius; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: carthamidin or isocarthamidin-derived intermediate(s); naringenin in single-enzyme assay
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: HSYA in coordination with CtCGT; 3-hydroxynaringenin (dihydrokaempferol) in standalone assay
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions as a 2OGD that, although capable of hydroxylating naringenin to 3-hydroxynaringenin in single-enzyme assay, primarily coordinates with CtCGT to catalyze the novel last-step reaction including dearomatization and di-C-glycosylation leading to HSYA.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: directly forms target product in coordination with CtCGT
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: flowers, especially budding flowers of safflower
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: clinical investigational new drug lead for acute ischemic stroke; multiple protective bioactivities
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for the final conversion to HSYA; silencing decreases HSYA accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Ct2OGD1 was identified from co-expression screening as an essential additional HSYA biosynthetic gene. 2. VIGS of Ct2OGD1 reduced HSYA content by 26.6% in safflower. 3. Ct2OGD1 enabled HSYA production in Nicotiana benthamiana only when combined with CtCGT and CtF6H. 4. Although previously annotated as F3H and capable of producing dihydrokaempferol from naringenin, the study showed its HSYA-relevant role is to coordinate with CtCGT in the last step. 5. Fe2+ and α-ketoglutarate were indispensable for the coupled HSYA-forming reaction.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Budding flowers in safflower; heterologously expressed in N. benthamiana leaves and yeast
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Co-expression screening; VIGS in Carthamus tinctorius; transient expression in Nicotiana benthamiana; in vitro enzyme assays; yeast semi-synthesis; SFLC; BiFC; MST
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Budding flower developmental stage; dependence on Fe2+, α-KG, and improved by ascorbic acid; VIGS; transient infiltration; substrate feeding
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key terminal-step enzyme, not explicitly established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Standalone activity toward naringenin can generate dihydrokaempferol, indicating possible competing catalytic capacity outside HSYA pathway
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential for reconstituting HSYA biosynthesis; should be stacked with CtCGT and CtF6H, with appropriate cofactors/support for 2OGD activity.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The gene had been reported previously as CtF3H, but was renamed Ct2OGD1 here to reflect its newly defined role in HSYA biosynthesis.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Carthamus tinctorius; Nicotiana benthamiana; Saccharomyces cerevisiae
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Intermediate isomerization step between carthamidin and isocarthamidin/carthamidin chalcone
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: isocarthamidin from carthamidin; facilitates interconversion via chalcone intermediate
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes isomerization between carthamidin and isocarthamidin, likely via carthamidin chalcone intermediate, thereby improving substrate availability for the final HSYA-forming step.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: flowers, especially budding flowers of safflower
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: clinical investigational new drug lead for acute ischemic stroke; multiple protective bioactivities
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves HSYA biosynthetic yield rather than being strictly indispensable under all tested contexts.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CtCHI1 was the only tested CHI homolog able to convert carthamidin to isocarthamidin. 2. It showed an expression pattern similar to CtF6H, CtCGT, and Ct2OGD1. 3. In yeast, addition of CtCHI1 increased HSYA yield about 20-fold when fed carthamidin. 4. In the purified multi-enzyme system, CtCHI1 increased HSYA yield 2.4-fold. 5. HSYA could also be generated from isocarthamidin, supporting CtCHI1's role in substrate interconversion.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in budding flowers, similar to other key HSYA genes
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Budding flowers in safflower; heterologously expressed in yeast and N. benthamiana-related assays
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Purified enzyme assay; yeast semi-synthesis; multi-enzyme in vitro catalysis; expression profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Budding flower developmental stage; substrate feeding with carthamidin/isocarthamidin
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: yield-enhancing supportive step; not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful accessory gene to boost HSYA yield in synthetic biology systems together with CtF6H, CtCGT, and Ct2OGD1.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Its transcript level decreased in CtF6H-, Ct2OGD1-, and CtCGT-VIGS groups, indicating coordinated expression among pathway genes.
